ORDER SUSTAINING THE DEMURRER BYT HE COMMISSIONER OF INSURANCE TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT DAVE JONES' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE THAT THE INSURANCE COMMISSIONER BREACHED A SPECIFIC MANDATORY DUTY. (SCHWARTZ V. POIZNER (2010) 187 CAL. APP. 4TH 572). A WRIT OF MANDATE CANNOT BE BASED ON GENERAL ENFORCEMENT PROVISIONS OR STATUTES INVOLVING THE DEFENDANT'S EXERCISE OF DISCRETION. PLAINTIFF ALSO FAILS TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING THAT THE DEFENDANT ABUSED HIS DISCRETION BY ACTING ARBITRARILY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: LESLIE C. NICHOLS, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO DISMISS, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES FILED BY DEFENDANT JONES, DAVE  COMMISSIONER OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE HEARING SET FOR NOV-15-2013 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FILED BY DEFENDANT JONES, DAVE  COMMISSIONER OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE
ORDER DISMISSING DEFENDANT INSURANCE COMMISSIONER WITH PREJUDICE
POS OF NTC OF ENTRY OF ORDER AND ORDER GRANTING MTN TO DISMISS FILED BY DEFENDANT JONES, DAVE  COMMISSIONER OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE
NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT DAVE JONES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO ALLEGE THAT THE INSURANCE COMMISSIONER BREACHED A SPECIFIC MANDATORY DUTY, WHICH THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT NOW DO. (SCHWARTZ V. POIZNER (2010) 187 CAL. APP. 4TH 572). A WRIT OF MANDATE CANNOT BE BASED ON GENERAL ENFORCEMENT PROVISIONS OR STATUTES INVOLVING THE DEFENDANT'S EXERCISE OF DISCRETION. PLAINTIFF ALSO FAILS TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING THAT THE DEFENDANT ABUSED HIS DISCRETION BY ACTING ARBITRARILY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
JURY FEES DEPOSITED BY PLAINTIFF CARLONI, ROBERT E.
REPLY TO OPPOS TO DEMURRER
MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-16-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER STIPULATED CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT AND PROTECTIVE ORDER. ENDORSED FILED COPIES ARE PLACED IN THE OUTBOX OF DEPARTMENT 301, NO SASE.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT DAVE JONES' MOTION TO DISMISS IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185
NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE OF TRIAL, JURY TRIAL SET FOR SEP-22-2014 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE ON JAN-08-2014 IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-21-2014 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-20-2013 Set For Trial On Sep-22-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss
Off Calendar Nov-15-2013 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ANNE MICHELLE BURR, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, 415.703.1403, FOR DEFENDANT DAVE JONES; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DAVE JONES' Motion To Dismiss IS Granted. No opposition filed. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-11-2013 Continued To Jan-08-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-26-2013 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ANNE MICHELLE BURR, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 703-1403 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DAVE JONES' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to allege that the Insurance Commissioner breached a specific mandatory duty. (Schwartz v. Poizner (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 572). A writ of mandate cannot be based on general enforcement provisions or statutes involving the defendant's exercise of discretion. Plaintiff also fails to allege facts showing that the defendant abused his discretion by acting arbitrarily. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/LCN)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-16-2013 Continued To Sep-26-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DAVE JONES' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued on the Court's own motion to September 26, 2013 at 9:30 a.m. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-12-2013 Continued To Oct-30-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-19-2013 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ANNE MICHELLE BURR, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 703-1403 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DAVE JONES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to allege that the Insurance Commissioner breached a specific mandatory duty, which the complaint does not now do. (Schwartz v. Poizner (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 572). A writ of mandate cannot be based on general enforcement provisions or statutes involving the defendant's exercise of discretion. Plaintiff also fails to allege facts showing that the defendant abused his discretion by acting arbitrarily. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
